key_insight: Elastic energy reduction can drive abnormal grain growth in ultrafine-grained Ni, selectively favoring grains with in-plane <100> orientation under cyclic elastic loading.
engineering_relevance: This suggests that even without plastic deformation or high temperatures, elastic anisotropy and loading conditions can trigger grain coarsening in grain-refined metals, affecting long-term microstructural stability.
method: Phase-field simulations of grain evolution under cyclic elastic loading
Micromechanical analysis of local stress states and grain geometry
Extension to materials with different elastic anisotropy
evidence_signal: Simulation results show AGG kinetics and indicate that local grain geometry and stress state govern the growth pathway, supporting elastic-energy-driven selective growth.
watch_item: The framework is mechanism-focused and should be validated experimentally across material systems and loading histories before being used for design decisions.
paper_title: Phase-field modeling of elastically driven abnormal grain growth
